   108→// Client allows reading and writing from/to a remote HTTP endpoint.
   109→type Client struct {
   110→	remoteName string // Used to differentiate clients in metrics.
   111→	urlString  string // url.String()
   112→	Client     *http.Client
   113→	timeout    time.Duration
   114→
   115→	retryOnRateLimit      bool
   116→	chunkedReadLimit      uint64
   117→	acceptedResponseTypes []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType
   118→
   119→	readQueries         prometheus.Gauge
   120→	readQueriesTotal    *prometheus.CounterVec
   121→	readQueriesDuration prometheus.ObserverVec
   122→
   123→	writeProtoMsg    remoteapi.WriteMessageType
   124→	writeCompression remoteapi.Compression
   125→}
   126→
   127→// ClientConfig configures a client.
   128→type ClientConfig struct {
   129→	URL                   *config_util.URL
   130→	Timeout               model.Duration
   131→	HTTPClientConfig      config_util.HTTPClientConfig
   132→	SigV4Config           *sigv4.SigV4Config
   133→	AzureADConfig         *azuread.AzureADConfig
   134→	GoogleIAMConfig       *googleiam.Config
   135→	Headers               map[string]string
   136→	RetryOnRateLimit      bool
   137→	WriteProtoMsg         remoteapi.WriteMessageType
   138→	ChunkedReadLimit      uint64
   139→	RoundRobinDNS         bool
   140→	AcceptedResponseTypes []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType
   141→}
   142→
   143→// ReadClient will request the STREAMED_XOR_CHUNKS method of remote read but can
   144→// also fall back to the SAMPLES method if necessary.
   145→type ReadClient interface {
   146→	Read(ctx context.Context, query *prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error)
   147→	ReadMultiple(ctx context.Context, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error)
   148→}
   149→
   150→// NewReadClient creates a new client for remote read.
   151→func NewReadClient(name string, conf *ClientConfig, optFuncs ...config_util.HTTPClientOption) (ReadClient, error) {
   152→	httpClient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(conf.HTTPClientConfig, "remote_storage_read_client", optFuncs...)
   153→	if err != nil {
   154→		return nil, err
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	t := httpClient.Transport
   158→	if len(conf.Headers) > 0 {
   159→		t = newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(conf.Headers, t)
   160→	}
   161→	httpClient.Transport = otelhttp.NewTransport(t)
   162→
   163→	// Set accepted response types, default to existing behavior if not specified.
   164→	acceptedResponseTypes := conf.AcceptedResponseTypes
   165→	if len(acceptedResponseTypes) == 0 {
   166→		acceptedResponseTypes = AcceptedResponseTypes
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	return &Client{
   170→		remoteName:            name,
   171→		urlString:             conf.URL.String(),
   172→		Client:                httpClient,
   173→		timeout:               time.Duration(conf.Timeout),
   174→		chunkedReadLimit:      conf.ChunkedReadLimit,
   175→		acceptedResponseTypes: acceptedResponseTypes,
   176→		readQueries:           remoteReadQueries.WithLabelValues(name, conf.URL.String()),
   177→		readQueriesTotal:      remoteReadQueriesTotal.MustCurryWith(prometheus.Labels{remoteName: name, endpoint: conf.URL.String()}),
   178→		readQueriesDuration:   remoteReadQueryDuration.MustCurryWith(prometheus.Labels{remoteName: name, endpoint: conf.URL.String()}),
   179→	}, nil
   180→}
   181→
   182→// NewWriteClient creates a new client for remote write.
   183→func NewWriteClient(name string, conf *ClientConfig) (WriteClient, error) {
   184→	var httpOpts []config_util.HTTPClientOption
   185→	if conf.RoundRobinDNS {
   186→		httpOpts = []config_util.HTTPClientOption{config_util.WithDialContextFunc(newDialContextWithRoundRobinDNS().dialContextFn())}
   187→	}
   188→	httpClient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(conf.HTTPClientConfig, "remote_storage_write_client", httpOpts...)
   189→	if err != nil {
   190→		return nil, err
   191→	}
   192→	t := httpClient.Transport
   193→
   194→	if len(conf.Headers) > 0 {
   195→		t = newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(conf.Headers, t)
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	if conf.SigV4Config != nil {
   199→		t, err = sigv4.NewSigV4RoundTripper(conf.SigV4Config, t)
   200→		if err != nil {
   201→			return nil, err
   202→		}
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	if conf.AzureADConfig != nil {
   206→		t, err = azuread.NewAzureADRoundTripper(conf.AzureADConfig, t)
   207→		if err != nil {
   208→			return nil, err
   209→		}
   210→	}
   211→
   212→	if conf.GoogleIAMConfig != nil {
   213→		t, err = googleiam.NewRoundTripper(conf.GoogleIAMConfig, t)
   214→		if err != nil {
   215→			return nil, err
   216→		}
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	writeProtoMsg := remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType
   220→	if conf.WriteProtoMsg != "" {
   221→		writeProtoMsg = conf.WriteProtoMsg
   222→	}
   223→	httpClient.Transport = otelhttp.NewTransport(
   224→		t,
   225→		otelhttp.WithClientTrace(func(ctx context.Context) *httptrace.ClientTrace {
   226→			return otelhttptrace.NewClientTrace(ctx, otelhttptrace.WithoutSubSpans())
   227→		}))
   228→
   229→	return &Client{
   230→		remoteName:       name,
   231→		urlString:        conf.URL.String(),
   232→		Client:           httpClient,
   233→		retryOnRateLimit: conf.RetryOnRateLimit,
   234→		timeout:          time.Duration(conf.Timeout),
   235→		writeProtoMsg:    writeProtoMsg,
   236→		writeCompression: remoteapi.SnappyBlockCompression,
   237→	}, nil
   238→}
   239→
   240→func newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(h map[string]string, underlyingRT http.RoundTripper) *injectHeadersRoundTripper {
   241→	return &injectHeadersRoundTripper{headers: h, RoundTripper: underlyingRT}
   242→}
   243→
   244→type injectHeadersRoundTripper struct {
   245→	headers map[string]string
   246→	http.RoundTripper
   247→}
   248→
   249→func (t *injectHeadersRoundTripper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
   250→	for key, value := range t.headers {
   251→		req.Header.Set(key, value)
   252→	}
   253→	return t.RoundTripper.RoundTrip(req)
   254→}
   255→
   256→const defaultBackoff = 0
   257→
   258→type RecoverableError struct {
   259→	error
   260→	retryAfter model.Duration
   261→}
   262→
   263→// Store sends a batch of samples to the HTTP endpoint, the request is the proto marshalled
   264→// and encoded bytes from codec.go.
   265→func (c *Client) Store(ctx context.Context, req []byte, attempt int) (WriteResponseStats, error) {
   266→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(req))
   267→	if err != nil {
   268→		// Errors from NewRequest are from unparsable URLs, so are not
   269→		// recoverable.
   270→		return WriteResponseStats{}, err
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	httpReq.Header.Add("Content-Encoding", string(c.writeCompression))
   274→	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", remoteWriteContentTypeHeaders[c.writeProtoMsg])
   275→	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", UserAgent)
   276→	if c.writeProtoMsg == remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType {
   277→		// Compatibility mode for 1.0.
   278→		httpReq.Header.Set(RemoteWriteVersionHeader, RemoteWriteVersion1HeaderValue)
   279→	} else {
   280→		httpReq.Header.Set(RemoteWriteVersionHeader, RemoteWriteVersion20HeaderValue)
   281→	}
   282→
   283→	if attempt > 0 {
   284→		httpReq.Header.Set("Retry-Attempt", strconv.Itoa(attempt))
   285→	}
   286→
   287→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.timeout)
   288→	defer cancel()
   289→
   290→	ctx, span := otel.Tracer("").Start(ctx, "Remote Store", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))
   291→	defer span.End()
   292→
   293→	httpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))
   294→	if err != nil {
   295→		// Errors from Client.Do are from (for example) network errors, so are
   296→		// recoverable.
   297→		return WriteResponseStats{}, RecoverableError{err, defaultBackoff}
   298→	}
   299→	defer func() {
   300→		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)
   301→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   302→	}()
   303→
   304→	// NOTE(bwplotka): Only PRW2 spec defines response HTTP headers. However, spec does not block
   305→	// PRW1 from sending them too for reliability. Support this case.
   306→	//
   307→	// TODO(bwplotka): Pass logger and emit debug on error?
   308→	// Parsing error means there were some response header values we can't parse,
   309→	// we can continue handling.
   310→	rs, _ := ParseWriteResponseStats(httpResp)
   311→
   312→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 == 2 {
   313→		return rs, nil
   314→	}
   315→
   316→	// Handling errors e.g. read potential error in the body.
   317→	// TODO(bwplotka): Pass logger and emit debug on error?
   318→	body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(httpResp.Body, maxErrMsgLen))
   319→	err = fmt.Errorf("server returned HTTP status %s: %s", httpResp.Status, body)
   320→
   321→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 == 5 ||
   322→		(c.retryOnRateLimit && httpResp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests) {
   323→		return rs, RecoverableError{err, retryAfterDuration(httpResp.Header.Get("Retry-After"))}
   324→	}
   325→	return rs, err
   326→}
   327→
   328→// retryAfterDuration returns the duration for the Retry-After header. In case of any errors, it
   329→// returns the defaultBackoff as if the header was never supplied.
   330→func retryAfterDuration(t string) model.Duration {
   331→	parsedDuration, err := time.Parse(http.TimeFormat, t)
   332→	if err == nil {
   333→		s := time.Until(parsedDuration).Seconds()
   334→		return model.Duration(s) * model.Duration(time.Second)
   335→	}
   336→	// The duration can be in seconds.
   337→	d, err := strconv.Atoi(t)
   338→	if err != nil {
   339→		return defaultBackoff
   340→	}
   341→	return model.Duration(d) * model.Duration(time.Second)
   342→}
   343→
   344→// Name uniquely identifies the client.
   345→func (c *Client) Name() string {
   346→	return c.remoteName
   347→}
   348→
   349→// Endpoint is the remote read or write endpoint.
   350→func (c *Client) Endpoint() string {
   351→	return c.urlString
   352→}
   353→
   354→// Read reads from a remote endpoint. The sortSeries parameter is only respected in the case of a samples response;
   355→// chunked responses arrive already sorted by the server.
   356→func (c *Client) Read(ctx context.Context, query *prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   357→	return c.ReadMultiple(ctx, []*prompb.Query{query}, sortSeries)
   358→}
   359→
   360→// ReadMultiple reads from a remote endpoint using multiple queries in a single request.
   361→// The sortSeries parameter is only respected in the case of a samples response;
   362→// chunked responses arrive already sorted by the server.
   363→// Returns a single SeriesSet with interleaved series from all queries.
   364→func (c *Client) ReadMultiple(ctx context.Context, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   365→	c.readQueries.Inc()
   366→	defer c.readQueries.Dec()
   367→
   368→	req := &prompb.ReadRequest{
   369→		Queries:               queries,
   370→		AcceptedResponseTypes: c.acceptedResponseTypes,
   371→	}
   372→
   373→	httpResp, cancel, start, err := c.executeReadRequest(ctx, req)
   374→	if err != nil {
   375→		return nil, err
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	return c.handleReadResponse(httpResp, req, queries, sortSeries, start, cancel)
   379→}
   380→
   381→// executeReadRequest creates and executes an HTTP request for reading data.
   382→func (c *Client) executeReadRequest(ctx context.Context, req *prompb.ReadRequest) (*http.Response, context.CancelFunc, time.Time, error) {
   383→	data, err := proto.Marshal(req)
   384→	if err != nil {
   385→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("unable to marshal read request: %w", err)
   386→	}
   387→
   388→	compressed := snappy.Encode(nil, data)
   389→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(compressed))
   390→	if err != nil {
   391→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("unable to create request: %w", err)
   392→	}
   393→	httpReq.Header.Add("Content-Encoding", "snappy")
   394→	httpReq.Header.Add("Accept-Encoding", "snappy")
   395→	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-protobuf")
   396→	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", UserAgent)
   397→	httpReq.Header.Set("X-Prometheus-Remote-Read-Version", "0.1.0")
   398→
   399→	errTimeout := fmt.Errorf("%w: request timed out after %s", context.DeadlineExceeded, c.timeout)
   400→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeoutCause(ctx, c.timeout, errTimeout)
   401→
   402→	ctx, span := otel.Tracer("").Start(ctx, "Remote Read", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))
   403→	defer span.End()
   404→
   405→	start := time.Now()
   406→	httpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))
   407→	if err != nil {
   408→		cancel()
   409→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("error sending request: %w", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	return httpResp, cancel, start, nil
   413→}
   414→
   415→// handleReadResponse processes the HTTP response and returns a SeriesSet.
   416→func (c *Client) handleReadResponse(httpResp *http.Response, req *prompb.ReadRequest, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool, start time.Time, cancel context.CancelFunc) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   417→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 != 2 {
   418→		// Make an attempt at getting an error message.
   419→		body, _ := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   420→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   421→
   422→		cancel()
   423→		errStr := strings.Trim(string(body), "\n")
   424→		err := errors.New(errStr)
   425→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("remote server %s returned http status %s: %w", c.urlString, httpResp.Status, err)
   426→	}
   427→
   428→	contentType := httpResp.Header.Get("Content-Type")
   429→
   430→	switch {
   431→	case strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "application/x-protobuf"):
   432→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("sampled").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   433→		c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("sampled", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()
   434→		ss, err := c.handleSampledResponse(req, httpResp, sortSeries)
   435→		cancel()
   436→		return ss, err
   437→	case strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "application/x-streamed-protobuf; proto=prometheus.ChunkedReadResponse"):
   438→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("chunked").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   439→
   440→		s := NewChunkedReader(httpResp.Body, c.chunkedReadLimit, nil)
   441→		return c.handleChunkedResponseImpl(s, httpResp, queries, func(err error) {
   442→			code := strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)
   443→			if !errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
   444→				code = "aborted_stream"
   445→			}
   446→			c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("chunked", code).Inc()
   447→			cancel()
   448→		}), nil
   449→	default:
   450→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("unsupported").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   451→		c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("unsupported", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()
   452→		cancel()
   453→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported content type: %s", contentType)
   454→	}
   455→}
   456→
   457→func (*Client) handleSampledResponse(req *prompb.ReadRequest, httpResp *http.Response, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   458→	compressed, err := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   459→	if err != nil {
   460→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response. HTTP status code: %s: %w", httpResp.Status, err)
   461→	}
   462→	defer func() {
   463→		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)
   464→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   465→	}()
   466→
   467→	uncompressed, err := snappy.Decode(nil, compressed)
   468→	if err != nil {
   469→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response: %w", err)
   470→	}
   471→
   472→	var resp prompb.ReadResponse
   473→	err = proto.Unmarshal(uncompressed, &resp)
   474→	if err != nil {
   475→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to unmarshal response body: %w", err)
   476→	}
   477→
   478→	if len(resp.Results) != len(req.Queries) {
   479→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("responses: want %d, got %d", len(req.Queries), len(resp.Results))
   480→	}
   481→
   482→	return combineQueryResults(resp.Results, sortSeries)
   483→}
   484→
   485→// combineQueryResults combines multiple query results into a single SeriesSet,
   486→// handling both sorted and unsorted cases appropriately.
   487→func combineQueryResults(results []*prompb.QueryResult, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   488→	if len(results) == 0 {
   489→		return &concreteSeriesSet{series: nil, cur: 0}, nil
   490→	}
   491→
   492→	if len(results) == 1 {
   493→		return FromQueryResult(sortSeries, results[0]), nil
   494→	}
   495→
   496→	// Multiple queries case - combine all results
   497→	if sortSeries {
   498→		// When sorting is requested, use MergeSeriesSet which can efficiently merge sorted inputs
   499→		var allSeriesSets []storage.SeriesSet
   500→		for _, result := range results {
   501→			seriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)
   502→			if err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {
   503→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading series from query result: %w", err)
   504→			}
   505→			allSeriesSets = append(allSeriesSets, seriesSet)
   506→		}
   507→		return storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(allSeriesSets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge), nil
   508→	}
   509→
   510→	// When sorting is not requested, just concatenate all series without using MergeSeriesSet
   511→	// since MergeSeriesSet requires sorted inputs
   512→	var allSeries []storage.Series
   513→	for _, result := range results {
   514→		seriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)
   515→		for seriesSet.Next() {
   516→			allSeries = append(allSeries, seriesSet.At())
   517→		}
   518→		if err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {
   519→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading series from query result: %w", err)
   520→		}
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	return &concreteSeriesSet{series: allSeries, cur: 0}, nil
   524→}
   525→
   526→// handleChunkedResponseImpl handles chunked responses for both single and multiple queries.
   527→func (*Client) handleChunkedResponseImpl(s *ChunkedReader, httpResp *http.Response, queries []*prompb.Query, onClose func(error)) storage.SeriesSet {
   528→	// For multiple queries in chunked response, we'll still use the existing infrastructure
   529→	// but we need to provide the timestamp range that covers all queries
   530→	var minStartTs, maxEndTs int64 = math.MaxInt64, math.MinInt64
   531→
   532→	for _, query := range queries {
   533→		minStartTs = min(minStartTs, query.StartTimestampMs)
   534→		maxEndTs = max(maxEndTs, query.EndTimestampMs)
   535→	}
   536→
   537→	return NewChunkedSeriesSet(s, httpResp.Body, minStartTs, maxEndTs, onClose)
   538→}

c.readQueries.Dec()\n\n\treq := &prompb.ReadRequest{\n\t\tQueries:               queries,\n\t\tAcceptedResponseTypes: c.acceptedResponseTypes,\n\t}\n\n\thttpResp, cancel, start, err := c.executeReadRequest(ctx, req)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, err\n\t}\n\n\treturn c.handleReadResponse(httpResp, req, queries, sortSeries, start, cancel)\n}\n\n// executeReadRequest creates and executes an HTTP request for reading data.\nfunc (c *Client) executeReadRequest(ctx context.Context, req *prompb.ReadRequest) (*http.Response, context.CancelFunc, time.Time, error) {\n\tdata, err := proto.Marshal(req)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to marshal read request: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tcompressed := snappy.Encode(nil, data)\n\thttpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(compressed))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to create request: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\thttpReq.Header.Add(\"Content-Encoding\", \"snappy\")\n\thttpReq.Header.Add(\"Accept-Encoding\", \"snappy\")\n\thttpReq.Header.Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/x-protobuf\")\n\thttpReq.Header.Set(\"User-Agent\", UserAgent)\n\thttpReq.Header.Set(\"X-Prometheus-Remote-Read-Version\", \"0.1.0\")\n\n\terrTimeout := fmt.Errorf(\"%w: request timed out after %s\", context.DeadlineExceeded, c.timeout)\n\tctx, cancel := context.WithTimeoutCause(ctx, c.timeout, errTimeout)\n\n\tctx, span := otel.Tracer(\"\").Start(ctx, \"Remote Read\", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))\n\tdefer span.End()\n\n\tstart := time.Now()\n\thttpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\tcancel()\n\t\treturn nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf(\"error sending request: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn httpResp, cancel, start, nil\n}\n\n// handleReadResponse processes the HTTP response and returns a SeriesSet.\nfunc (c *Client) handleReadResponse(httpResp *http.Response, req *prompb.ReadRequest, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool, start time.Time, cancel context.CancelFunc) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {\n\tif httpResp.StatusCode/100 != 2 {\n\t\t// Make an attempt at getting an error message.\n\t\tbody, _ := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)\n\t\t_ = httpResp.Body.Close()\n\n\t\tcancel()\n\t\terrStr := strings.Trim(string(body), \"\\n\")\n\t\terr := errors.New(errStr)\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"remote server %s returned http status %s: %w\", c.urlString, httpResp.Status, err)\n\t}\n\n\tcontentType := httpResp.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n\n\tswitch {\n\tcase strings.HasPrefix(contentType, \"application/x-protobuf\"):\n\t\tc.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues(\"sampled\").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())\n\t\tc.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues(\"sampled\", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()\n\t\tss, err := c.handleSampledResponse(req, httpResp, sortSeries)\n\t\tcancel()\n\t\treturn ss, err\n\tcase strings.HasPrefix(contentType, \"application/x-streamed-protobuf; proto=prometheus.ChunkedReadResponse\"):\n\t\tc.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues(\"chunked\").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())\n\n\t\ts := NewChunkedReader(httpResp.Body, c.chunkedReadLimit, nil)\n\t\treturn c.handleChunkedResponseImpl(s, httpResp, queries, func(err error) {\n\t\t\tcode := strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)\n\t\t\tif !errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {\n\t\t\t\tcode = \"aborted_stream\"\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tc.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues(\"chunked\", code).Inc()\n\t\t\tcancel()\n\t\t}), nil\n\tdefault:\n\t\tc.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues(\"unsupported\").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())\n\t\tc.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues(\"unsupported\", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()\n\t\tcancel()\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unsupported content type: %s\", contentType)\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (*Client) handleSampledResponse(req *prompb.ReadRequest, httpResp *http.Response, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {\n\tcompressed, err := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading response. HTTP status code: %s: %w\", httpResp.Status, err)\n\t}\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\t_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)\n\t\t_ = httpResp.Body.Close()\n\t}()\n\n\tuncompressed, err := snappy.Decode(nil, compressed)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading response: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tvar resp prompb.ReadResponse\n\terr = proto.Unmarshal(uncompressed, &resp)\n\tif err != nil {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"unable to unmarshal response body: %w\", err)\n\t}\n\n\tif len(resp.Results) != len(req.Queries) {\n\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"responses: want %d, got %d\", len(req.Queries), len(resp.Results))\n\t}\n\n\treturn combineQueryResults(resp.Results, sortSeries)\n}\n\n// combineQueryResults combines multiple query results into a single SeriesSet,\n// handling both sorted and unsorted cases appropriately.\nfunc combineQueryResults(results []*prompb.QueryResult, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {\n\tif len(results) == 0 {\n\t\treturn &concreteSeriesSet{series: nil, cur: 0}, nil\n\t}\n\n\tif len(results) == 1 {\n\t\treturn FromQueryResult(sortSeries, results[0]), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// Multiple queries case - combine all results\n\tif sortSeries {\n\t\t// When sorting is requested, use MergeSeriesSet which can efficiently merge sorted inputs\n\t\tvar allSeriesSets []storage.SeriesSet\n\t\tfor _, result := range results {\n\t\t\tseriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)\n\t\t\tif err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading series from query result: %w\", err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\tallSeriesSets = append(allSeriesSets, seriesSet)\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(allSeriesSets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge), nil\n\t}\n\n\t// When sorting is not requested, just concatenate all series without using MergeSeriesSet\n\t// since MergeSeriesSet requires sorted inputs\n\tvar allSeries []storage.Series\n\tfor _, result := range results {\n\t\tseriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)\n\t\tfor seriesSet.Next() {\n\t\t\tallSeries = append(allSeries, seriesSet.At())\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {\n\t\t\treturn nil, fmt.Errorf(\"error reading series from query result: %w\", err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn &concreteSeriesSet{series: allSeries, cur: 0}, nil\n}\n\n// handleChunkedResponseImpl handles chunked responses for both single and multiple queries.\nfunc (*Client) handleChunkedResponseImpl(s *ChunkedReader, httpResp *http.Response, queries []*prompb.Query, onClose func(error)) storage.SeriesSet {\n\t// For multiple queries in chunked response, we'll still use the existing infrastructure\n\t// but we need to provide the timestamp range that covers all queries\n\tvar minStartTs, maxEndTs int64 = math.MaxInt64, math.MinInt64\n\n\tfor _, query := range queries {\n\t\tminStartTs = min(minStartTs, query.StartTimestampMs)\n\t\tmaxEndTs = max(maxEndTs, query.EndTimestampMs)\n\t}\n\n\treturn NewChunkedSeriesSet(s, httpResp.Body, minStartTs, maxEndTs, onClose)\n}\n", "numLines": 539, "startLine": 1, "totalLines": 539}}

    61→	remoteWriteContentTypeHeaders = map[remoteapi.WriteMessageType]string{

   107→
   108→// Client allows reading and writing from/to a remote HTTP endpoint.
   109→type Client struct {
   110→	remoteName string // Used to differentiate clients in metrics.
   111→	urlString  string // url.String()
   112→	Client     *http.Client
   113→	timeout    time.Duration
   114→
   115→	retryOnRateLimit      bool
   116→	chunkedReadLimit      uint64
   117→	acceptedResponseTypes []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType
   118→
   119→	readQueries         prometheus.Gauge
   120→	readQueriesTotal    *prometheus.CounterVec
   121→	readQueriesDuration prometheus.ObserverVec
   122→
   123→	writeProtoMsg    remoteapi.WriteMessageType
   124→	writeCompression remoteapi.Compression
   125→}
   126→
   127→// ClientConfig configures a client.
   128→type ClientConfig struct {
   129→	URL                   *config_util.URL
   130→	Timeout               model.Duration
   131→	HTTPClientConfig      config_util.HTTPClientConfig
   132→	SigV4Config           *sigv4.SigV4Config
   133→	AzureADConfig         *azuread.AzureADConfig
   134→	GoogleIAMConfig       *googleiam.Config
   135→	Headers               map[string]string
   136→	RetryOnRateLimit      bool
   137→	WriteProtoMsg         remoteapi.WriteMessageType
   138→	ChunkedReadLimit      uint64
   139→	RoundRobinDNS         bool
   140→	AcceptedResponseTypes []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType
   141→}
   142→
   143→// ReadClient will request the STREAMED_XOR_CHUNKS method of remote read but can
   144→// also fall back to the SAMPLES method if necessary.
   145→type ReadClient interface {
   146→	Read(ctx context.Context, query *prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error)
   147→	ReadMultiple(ctx context.Context, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error)
   148→}
   149→
   150→// NewReadClient creates a new client for remote read.
   151→func NewReadClient(name string, conf *ClientConfig, optFuncs ...config_util.HTTPClientOption) (ReadClient, error) {
   152→	httpClient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(conf.HTTPClientConfig, "remote_storage_read_client", optFuncs...)
   153→	if err != nil {
   154→		return nil, err
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	t := httpClient.Transport
   158→	if len(conf.Headers) > 0 {
   159→		t = newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(conf.Headers, t)
   160→	}
   161→	httpClient.Transport = otelhttp.NewTransport(t)
   162→
   163→	// Set accepted response types, default to existing behavior if not specified.
   164→	acceptedResponseTypes := conf.AcceptedResponseTypes
   165→	if len(acceptedResponseTypes) == 0 {
   166→		acceptedResponseTypes = AcceptedResponseTypes
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	return &Client{
   170→		remoteName:            name,
   171→		urlString:             conf.URL.String(),
   172→		Client:                httpClient,
   173→		timeout:               time.Duration(conf.Timeout),
   174→		chunkedReadLimit:      conf.ChunkedReadLimit,
   175→		acceptedResponseTypes: acceptedResponseTypes,
   176→		readQueries:           remoteReadQueries.WithLabelValues(name, conf.URL.String()),
   177→		readQueriesTotal:      remoteReadQueriesTotal.MustCurryWith(prometheus.Labels{remoteName: name, endpoint: conf.URL.String()}),
   178→		readQueriesDuration:   remoteReadQueryDuration.MustCurryWith(prometheus.Labels{remoteName: name, endpoint: conf.URL.String()}),
   179→	}, nil
   180→}
   181→
   182→// NewWriteClient creates a new client for remote write.
   183→func NewWriteClient(name string, conf *ClientConfig) (WriteClient, error) {
   184→	var httpOpts []config_util.HTTPClientOption
   185→	if conf.RoundRobinDNS {
   186→		httpOpts = []config_util.HTTPClientOption{config_util.WithDialContextFunc(newDialContextWithRoundRobinDNS().dialContextFn())}
   187→	}
   188→	httpClient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(conf.HTTPClientConfig, "remote_storage_write_client", httpOpts...)
   189→	if err != nil {
   190→		return nil, err
   191→	}
   192→	t := httpClient.Transport
   193→
   194→	if len(conf.Headers) > 0 {
   195→		t = newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(conf.Headers, t)
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	if conf.SigV4Config != nil {
   199→		t, err = sigv4.NewSigV4RoundTripper(conf.SigV4Config, t)
   200→		if err != nil {
   201→			return nil, err
   202→		}
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	if conf.AzureADConfig != nil {
   206→		t, err = azuread.NewAzureADRoundTripper(conf.AzureADConfig, t)
   207→		if err != nil {
   208→			return nil, err
   209→		}
   210→	}
   211→
   212→	if conf.GoogleIAMConfig != nil {
   213→		t, err = googleiam.NewRoundTripper(conf.GoogleIAMConfig, t)
   214→		if err != nil {
   215→			return nil, err
   216→		}
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	writeProtoMsg := remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType
   220→	if conf.WriteProtoMsg != "" {
   221→		writeProtoMsg = conf.WriteProtoMsg
   222→	}
   223→	httpClient.Transport = otelhttp.NewTransport(
   224→		t,
   225→		otelhttp.WithClientTrace(func(ctx context.Context) *httptrace.ClientTrace {
   226→			return otelhttptrace.NewClientTrace(ctx, otelhttptrace.WithoutSubSpans())
   227→		}))
   228→
   229→	return &Client{
   230→		remoteName:       name,
   231→		urlString:        conf.URL.String(),
   232→		Client:           httpClient,
   233→		retryOnRateLimit: conf.RetryOnRateLimit,
   234→		timeout:          time.Duration(conf.Timeout),
   235→		writeProtoMsg:    writeProtoMsg,
   236→		writeCompression: remoteapi.SnappyBlockCompression,
   237→	}, nil
   238→}
   239→
   240→func newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(h map[string]string, underlyingRT http.RoundTripper) *injectHeadersRoundTripper {
   241→	return &injectHeadersRoundTripper{headers: h, RoundTripper: underlyingRT}
   242→}
   243→
   244→type injectHeadersRoundTripper struct {
   245→	headers map[string]string
   246→	http.RoundTripper
   247→}
   248→
   249→func (t *injectHeadersRoundTripper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
   250→	for key, value := range t.headers {
   251→		req.Header.Set(key, value)
   252→	}
   253→	return t.RoundTripper.RoundTrip(req)
   254→}
   255→
   256→const defaultBackoff = 0
   257→
   258→type RecoverableError struct {
   259→	error
   260→	retryAfter model.Duration
   261→}
   262→
   263→// Store sends a batch of samples to the HTTP endpoint, the request is the proto marshalled
   264→// and encoded bytes from codec.go.
   265→func (c *Client) Store(ctx context.Context, req []byte, attempt int) (WriteResponseStats, error) {
   266→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(req))
   267→	if err != nil {
   268→		// Errors from NewRequest are from unparsable URLs, so are not
   269→		// recoverable.
   270→		return WriteResponseStats{}, err
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	httpReq.Header.Add("Content-Encoding", string(c.writeCompression))
   274→	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", remoteWriteContentTypeHeaders[c.writeProtoMsg])
   275→	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", UserAgent)
   276→	if c.writeProtoMsg == remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType {
   277→		// Compatibility mode for 1.0.
   278→		httpReq.Header.Set(RemoteWriteVersionHeader, RemoteWriteVersion1HeaderValue)
   279→	} else {
   280→		httpReq.Header.Set(RemoteWriteVersionHeader, RemoteWriteVersion20HeaderValue)
   281→	}
   282→
   283→	if attempt > 0 {
   284→		httpReq.Header.Set("Retry-Attempt", strconv.Itoa(attempt))
   285→	}
   286→
   287→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.timeout)
   288→	defer cancel()
   289→
   290→	ctx, span := otel.Tracer("").Start(ctx, "Remote Store", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))
   291→	defer span.End()
   292→
   293→	httpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))
   294→	if err != nil {
   295→		// Errors from Client.Do are from (for example) network errors, so are
   296→		// recoverable.
   297→		return WriteResponseStats{}, RecoverableError{err, defaultBackoff}
   298→	}
   299→	defer func() {
   300→		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)
   301→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   302→	}()
   303→
   304→	// NOTE(bwplotka): Only PRW2 spec defines response HTTP headers. However, spec does not block
   305→	// PRW1 from sending them too for reliability. Support this case.
   306→	//
   307→	// TODO(bwplotka): Pass logger and emit debug on error?
   308→	// Parsing error means there were some response header values we can't parse,
   309→	// we can continue handling.
   310→	rs, _ := ParseWriteResponseStats(httpResp)
   311→
   312→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 == 2 {
   313→		return rs, nil
   314→	}
   315→
   316→	// Handling errors e.g. read potential error in the body.
   317→	// TODO(bwplotka): Pass logger and emit debug on error?
   318→	body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(httpResp.Body, maxErrMsgLen))
   319→	err = fmt.Errorf("server returned HTTP status %s: %s", httpResp.Status, body)
   320→
   321→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 == 5 ||
   322→		(c.retryOnRateLimit && httpResp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests) {
   323→		return rs, RecoverableError{err, retryAfterDuration(httpResp.Header.Get("Retry-After"))}
   324→	}
   325→	return rs, err
   326→}
   327→
   328→// retryAfterDuration returns the duration for the Retry-After header. In case of any errors, it
   329→// returns the defaultBackoff as if the header was never supplied.
   330→func retryAfterDuration(t string) model.Duration {
   331→	parsedDuration, err := time.Parse(http.TimeFormat, t)
   332→	if err == nil {
   333→		s := time.Until(parsedDuration).Seconds()
   334→		return model.Duration(s) * model.Duration(time.Second)
   335→	}
   336→	// The duration can be in seconds.
   337→	d, err := strconv.Atoi(t)
   338→	if err != nil {
   339→		return defaultBackoff
   340→	}
   341→	return model.Duration(d) * model.Duration(time.Second)
   342→}
   343→
   344→// Name uniquely identifies the client.
   345→func (c *Client) Name() string {
   346→	return c.remoteName
   347→}
   348→
   349→// Endpoint is the remote read or write endpoint.
   350→func (c *Client) Endpoint() string {
   351→	return c.urlString
   352→}
   353→
   354→// Read reads from a remote endpoint. The sortSeries parameter is only respected in the case of a samples response;
   355→// chunked responses arrive already sorted by the server.
   356→func (c *Client) Read(ctx context.Context, query *prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   357→	return c.ReadMultiple(ctx, []*prompb.Query{query}, sortSeries)
   358→}
   359→
   360→// ReadMultiple reads from a remote endpoint using multiple queries in a single request.
   361→// The sortSeries parameter is only respected in the case of a samples response;
   362→// chunked responses arrive already sorted by the server.
   363→// Returns a single SeriesSet with interleaved series from all queries.
   364→func (c *Client) ReadMultiple(ctx context.Context, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   365→	c.readQueries.Inc()
   366→	defer c.readQueries.Dec()
   367→
   368→	req := &prompb.ReadRequest{
   369→		Queries:               queries,
   370→		AcceptedResponseTypes: c.acceptedResponseTypes,
   371→	}
   372→
   373→	httpResp, cancel, start, err := c.executeReadRequest(ctx, req)
   374→	if err != nil {
   375→		return nil, err
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	return c.handleReadResponse(httpResp, req, queries, sortSeries, start, cancel)
   379→}
   380→
   381→// executeReadRequest creates and executes an HTTP request for reading data.
   382→func (c *Client) executeReadRequest(ctx context.Context, req *prompb.ReadRequest) (*http.Response, context.CancelFunc, time.Time, error) {
   383→	data, err := proto.Marshal(req)
   384→	if err != nil {
   385→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("unable to marshal read request: %w", err)
   386→	}
   387→
   388→	compressed := snappy.Encode(nil, data)
   389→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(compressed))
   390→	if err != nil {
   391→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("unable to create request: %w", err)
   392→	}
   393→	httpReq.Header.Add("Content-Encoding", "snappy")
   394→	httpReq.Header.Add("Accept-Encoding", "snappy")
   395→	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-protobuf")
   396→	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", UserAgent)
   397→	httpReq.Header.Set("X-Prometheus-Remote-Read-Version", "0.1.0")
   398→
   399→	errTimeout := fmt.Errorf("%w: request timed out after %s", context.DeadlineExceeded, c.timeout)
   400→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeoutCause(ctx, c.timeout, errTimeout)
   401→
   402→	ctx, span := otel.Tracer("").Start(ctx, "Remote Read", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))
   403→	defer span.End()
   404→
   405→	start := time.Now()
   406→	httpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))
   407→	if err != nil {
   408→		cancel()
   409→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("error sending request: %w", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	return httpResp, cancel, start, nil
   413→}
   414→
   415→// handleReadResponse processes the HTTP response and returns a SeriesSet.
   416→func (c *Client) handleReadResponse(httpResp *http.Response, req *prompb.ReadRequest, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool, start time.Time, cancel context.CancelFunc) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   417→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 != 2 {
   418→		// Make an attempt at getting an error message.
   419→		body, _ := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   420→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   421→
   422→		cancel()
   423→		errStr := strings.Trim(string(body), "\n")
   424→		err := errors.New(errStr)
   425→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("remote server %s returned http status %s: %w", c.urlString, httpResp.Status, err)
   426→	}
   427→
   428→	contentType := httpResp.Header.Get("Content-Type")
   429→
   430→	switch {
   431→	case strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "application/x-protobuf"):
   432→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("sampled").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   433→		c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("sampled", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()
   434→		ss, err := c.handleSampledResponse(req, httpResp, sortSeries)
   435→		cancel()
   436→		return ss, err
   437→	case strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "application/x-streamed-protobuf; proto=prometheus.ChunkedReadResponse"):
   438→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("chunked").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   439→
   440→		s := NewChunkedReader(httpResp.Body, c.chunkedReadLimit, nil)
   441→		return c.handleChunkedResponseImpl(s, httpResp, queries, func(err error) {
   442→			code := strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)
   443→			if !errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
   444→				code = "aborted_stream"
   445→			}
   446→			c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("chunked", code).Inc()
   447→			cancel()
   448→		}), nil
   449→	default:
   450→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("unsupported").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   451→		c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("unsupported", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()
   452→		cancel()
   453→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported content type: %s", contentType)
   454→	}
   455→}
   456→
   457→func (*Client) handleSampledResponse(req *prompb.ReadRequest, httpResp *http.Response, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   458→	compressed, err := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   459→	if err != nil {
   460→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response. HTTP status code: %s: %w", httpResp.Status, err)
   461→	}
   462→	defer func() {
   463→		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)
   464→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   465→	}()
   466→
   467→	uncompressed, err := snappy.Decode(nil, compressed)
   468→	if err != nil {
   469→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response: %w", err)
   470→	}
   471→
   472→	var resp prompb.ReadResponse
   473→	err = proto.Unmarshal(uncompressed, &resp)
   474→	if err != nil {
   475→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to unmarshal response body: %w", err)
   476→	}
   477→
   478→	if len(resp.Results) != len(req.Queries) {
   479→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("responses: want %d, got %d", len(req.Queries), len(resp.Results))
   480→	}
   481→
   482→	return combineQueryResults(resp.Results, sortSeries)
   483→}
   484→
   485→// combineQueryResults combines multiple query results into a single SeriesSet,
   486→// handling both sorted and unsorted cases appropriately.
   487→func combineQueryResults(results []*prompb.QueryResult, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   488→	if len(results) == 0 {
   489→		return &concreteSeriesSet{series: nil, cur: 0}, nil
   490→	}
   491→
   492→	if len(results) == 1 {
   493→		return FromQueryResult(sortSeries, results[0]), nil
   494→	}
   495→
   496→	// Multiple queries case - combine all results
   497→	if sortSeries {
   498→		// When sorting is requested, use MergeSeriesSet which can efficiently merge sorted inputs
   499→		var allSeriesSets []storage.SeriesSet
   500→		for _, result := range results {
   501→			seriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)
   502→			if err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {
   503→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading series from query result: %w", err)
   504→			}
   505→			allSeriesSets = append(allSeriesSets, seriesSet)
   506→		}
   507→		return storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(allSeriesSets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge), nil
   508→	}
   509→
   510→	// When sorting is not requested, just concatenate all series without using MergeSeriesSet
   511→	// since MergeSeriesSet requires sorted inputs
   512→	var allSeries []storage.Series
   513→	for _, result := range results {
   514→		seriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)
   515→		for seriesSet.Next() {
   516→			allSeries = append(allSeries, seriesSet.At())
   517→		}
   518→		if err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {
   519→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading series from query result: %w", err)
   520→		}
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	return &concreteSeriesSet{series: allSeries, cur: 0}, nil
   524→}
   525→
   526→// handleChunkedResponseImpl handles chunked responses for both single and multiple queries.
   527→func (*Client) handleChunkedResponseImpl(s *ChunkedReader, httpResp *http.Response, queries []*prompb.Query, onClose func(error)) storage.SeriesSet {
   528→	// For multiple queries in chunked response, we'll still use the existing infrastructure
   529→	// but we need to provide the timestamp range that covers all queries
   530→	var minStartTs, maxEndTs int64 = math.MaxInt64, math.MinInt64
   531→
   532→	for _, query := range queries {
   533→		minStartTs = min(minStartTs, query.StartTimestampMs)
   534→		maxEndTs = max(maxEndTs, query.EndTimestampMs)
   535→	}
   536→
   537→	return NewChunkedSeriesSet(s, httpResp.Body, minStartTs, maxEndTs, onClose)
   538→}
   539→

    48→const (
    49→	maxErrMsgLen = 1024
    50→
    51→	RemoteWriteVersionHeader        = "X-Prometheus-Remote-Write-Version"
    52→	RemoteWriteVersion1HeaderValue  = "0.1.0"
    53→	RemoteWriteVersion20HeaderValue = "2.0.0"
    54→	appProtoContentType             = "application/x-protobuf"

    57→var (
    58→	// UserAgent represents Prometheus version to use for user agent header.

    60→
    61→	remoteWriteContentTypeHeaders = map[remoteapi.WriteMessageType]string{
    62→		remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType: appProtoContentType, // Also application/x-protobuf;proto=prometheus.WriteRequest but simplified for compatibility with 1.x spec.
    63→		remoteapi.WriteV2MessageType: appProtoContentType + ";proto=io.prometheus.write.v2.Request",
    64→	}
    65→
    66→	AcceptedResponseTypes = []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType{
    67→		prompb.ReadRequest_STREAMED_XOR_CHUNKS,
    68→		prompb.ReadRequest_SAMPLES,
    69→	}
    70→
    71→	remoteReadQueriesTotal = prometheus.NewCounterVec(
    72→		prometheus.CounterOpts{
    73→			Namespace: namespace,
    74→			Subsystem: "remote_read_client",
    75→			Name:      "queries_total",
    76→			Help:      "The total number of remote read queries.",
    77→		},
    78→		[]string{remoteName, endpoint, "response_type", "code"},
    79→	)
    80→	remoteReadQueries = prometheus.NewGaugeVec(
    81→		prometheus.GaugeOpts{
    82→			Namespace: namespace,
    83→			Subsystem: "remote_read_client",
    84→			Name:      "queries",
    85→			Help:      "The number of in-flight remote read queries.",
    86→		},
    87→		[]string{remoteName, endpoint},
    88→	)
    89→	remoteReadQueryDuration = prometheus.NewHistogramVec(
    90→		prometheus.HistogramOpts{
    91→			Namespace:                       namespace,
    92→			Subsystem:                       "remote_read_client",
    93→			Name:                            "request_duration_seconds",
    94→			Help:                            "Histogram of the latency for remote read requests. Note that for streamed responses this is only the duration of the initial call and does not include the processing of the stream.",
    95→			Buckets:                         append(prometheus.DefBuckets, 25, 60),
    96→			NativeHistogramBucketFactor:     1.1,
    97→			NativeHistogramMaxBucketNumber:  100,
    98→			NativeHistogramMinResetDuration: 1 * time.Hour,
    99→		},
   100→		[]string{remoteName, endpoint, "response_type"},
   101→	)
   102→)
   103→
   104→func init() {
   105→	prometheus.MustRegister(remoteReadQueriesTotal, remoteReadQueries, remoteReadQueryDuration)
   106→}
   107→
   108→// Client allows reading and writing from/to a remote HTTP endpoint.
   109→type Client struct {
   110→	remoteName string // Used to differentiate clients in metrics.
   111→	urlString  string // url.String()
   112→	Client     *http.Client
   113→	timeout    time.Duration
   114→
   115→	retryOnRateLimit      bool
   116→	chunkedReadLimit      uint64
   117→	acceptedResponseTypes []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType
   118→
   119→	readQueries         prometheus.Gauge
   120→	readQueriesTotal    *prometheus.CounterVec
   121→	readQueriesDuration prometheus.ObserverVec
   122→
   123→	writeProtoMsg    remoteapi.WriteMessageType
   124→	writeCompression remoteapi.Compression
   125→}
   126→
   127→// ClientConfig configures a client.
   128→type ClientConfig struct {
   129→	URL                   *config_util.URL
   130→	Timeout               model.Duration
   131→	HTTPClientConfig      config_util.HTTPClientConfig
   132→	SigV4Config           *sigv4.SigV4Config
   133→	AzureADConfig         *azuread.AzureADConfig
   134→	GoogleIAMConfig       *googleiam.Config
   135→	Headers               map[string]string
   136→	RetryOnRateLimit      bool
   137→	WriteProtoMsg         remoteapi.WriteMessageType
   138→	ChunkedReadLimit      uint64
   139→	RoundRobinDNS         bool
   140→	AcceptedResponseTypes []prompb.ReadRequest_ResponseType
   141→}
   142→
   143→// ReadClient will request the STREAMED_XOR_CHUNKS method of remote read but can
   144→// also fall back to the SAMPLES method if necessary.
   145→type ReadClient interface {
   146→	Read(ctx context.Context, query *prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error)
   147→	ReadMultiple(ctx context.Context, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error)
   148→}
   149→
   150→// NewReadClient creates a new client for remote read.
   151→func NewReadClient(name string, conf *ClientConfig, optFuncs ...config_util.HTTPClientOption) (ReadClient, error) {
   152→	httpClient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(conf.HTTPClientConfig, "remote_storage_read_client", optFuncs...)
   153→	if err != nil {
   154→		return nil, err
   155→	}
   156→
   157→	t := httpClient.Transport
   158→	if len(conf.Headers) > 0 {
   159→		t = newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(conf.Headers, t)
   160→	}
   161→	httpClient.Transport = otelhttp.NewTransport(t)
   162→
   163→	// Set accepted response types, default to existing behavior if not specified.
   164→	acceptedResponseTypes := conf.AcceptedResponseTypes
   165→	if len(acceptedResponseTypes) == 0 {
   166→		acceptedResponseTypes = AcceptedResponseTypes
   167→	}
   168→
   169→	return &Client{
   170→		remoteName:            name,
   171→		urlString:             conf.URL.String(),
   172→		Client:                httpClient,
   173→		timeout:               time.Duration(conf.Timeout),
   174→		chunkedReadLimit:      conf.ChunkedReadLimit,
   175→		acceptedResponseTypes: acceptedResponseTypes,
   176→		readQueries:           remoteReadQueries.WithLabelValues(name, conf.URL.String()),
   177→		readQueriesTotal:      remoteReadQueriesTotal.MustCurryWith(prometheus.Labels{remoteName: name, endpoint: conf.URL.String()}),
   178→		readQueriesDuration:   remoteReadQueryDuration.MustCurryWith(prometheus.Labels{remoteName: name, endpoint: conf.URL.String()}),
   179→	}, nil
   180→}
   181→
   182→// NewWriteClient creates a new client for remote write.
   183→func NewWriteClient(name string, conf *ClientConfig) (WriteClient, error) {
   184→	var httpOpts []config_util.HTTPClientOption
   185→	if conf.RoundRobinDNS {
   186→		httpOpts = []config_util.HTTPClientOption{config_util.WithDialContextFunc(newDialContextWithRoundRobinDNS().dialContextFn())}
   187→	}
   188→	httpClient, err := config_util.NewClientFromConfig(conf.HTTPClientConfig, "remote_storage_write_client", httpOpts...)
   189→	if err != nil {
   190→		return nil, err
   191→	}
   192→	t := httpClient.Transport
   193→
   194→	if len(conf.Headers) > 0 {
   195→		t = newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(conf.Headers, t)
   196→	}
   197→
   198→	if conf.SigV4Config != nil {
   199→		t, err = sigv4.NewSigV4RoundTripper(conf.SigV4Config, t)
   200→		if err != nil {
   201→			return nil, err
   202→		}
   203→	}
   204→
   205→	if conf.AzureADConfig != nil {
   206→		t, err = azuread.NewAzureADRoundTripper(conf.AzureADConfig, t)
   207→		if err != nil {
   208→			return nil, err
   209→		}
   210→	}
   211→
   212→	if conf.GoogleIAMConfig != nil {
   213→		t, err = googleiam.NewRoundTripper(conf.GoogleIAMConfig, t)
   214→		if err != nil {
   215→			return nil, err
   216→		}
   217→	}
   218→
   219→	writeProtoMsg := remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType
   220→	if conf.WriteProtoMsg != "" {
   221→		writeProtoMsg = conf.WriteProtoMsg
   222→	}
   223→	httpClient.Transport = otelhttp.NewTransport(
   224→		t,
   225→		otelhttp.WithClientTrace(func(ctx context.Context) *httptrace.ClientTrace {
   226→			return otelhttptrace.NewClientTrace(ctx, otelhttptrace.WithoutSubSpans())
   227→		}))
   228→
   229→	return &Client{
   230→		remoteName:       name,
   231→		urlString:        conf.URL.String(),
   232→		Client:           httpClient,
   233→		retryOnRateLimit: conf.RetryOnRateLimit,
   234→		timeout:          time.Duration(conf.Timeout),
   235→		writeProtoMsg:    writeProtoMsg,
   236→		writeCompression: remoteapi.SnappyBlockCompression,
   237→	}, nil
   238→}
   239→
   240→func newInjectHeadersRoundTripper(h map[string]string, underlyingRT http.RoundTripper) *injectHeadersRoundTripper {
   241→	return &injectHeadersRoundTripper{headers: h, RoundTripper: underlyingRT}
   242→}
   243→
   244→type injectHeadersRoundTripper struct {
   245→	headers map[string]string
   246→	http.RoundTripper
   247→}
   248→
   249→func (t *injectHeadersRoundTripper) RoundTrip(req *http.Request) (*http.Response, error) {
   250→	for key, value := range t.headers {
   251→		req.Header.Set(key, value)
   252→	}
   253→	return t.RoundTripper.RoundTrip(req)
   254→}
   255→
   256→const defaultBackoff = 0
   257→
   258→type RecoverableError struct {
   259→	error
   260→	retryAfter model.Duration
   261→}
   262→
   263→// Store sends a batch of samples to the HTTP endpoint, the request is the proto marshalled
   264→// and encoded bytes from codec.go.
   265→func (c *Client) Store(ctx context.Context, req []byte, attempt int) (WriteResponseStats, error) {
   266→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(req))
   267→	if err != nil {
   268→		// Errors from NewRequest are from unparsable URLs, so are not
   269→		// recoverable.
   270→		return WriteResponseStats{}, err
   271→	}
   272→
   273→	httpReq.Header.Add("Content-Encoding", string(c.writeCompression))
   274→	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", remoteWriteContentTypeHeaders[c.writeProtoMsg])
   275→	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", UserAgent)
   276→	if c.writeProtoMsg == remoteapi.WriteV1MessageType {
   277→		// Compatibility mode for 1.0.
   278→		httpReq.Header.Set(RemoteWriteVersionHeader, RemoteWriteVersion1HeaderValue)
   279→	} else {
   280→		httpReq.Header.Set(RemoteWriteVersionHeader, RemoteWriteVersion20HeaderValue)
   281→	}
   282→
   283→	if attempt > 0 {
   284→		httpReq.Header.Set("Retry-Attempt", strconv.Itoa(attempt))
   285→	}
   286→
   287→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(ctx, c.timeout)
   288→	defer cancel()
   289→
   290→	ctx, span := otel.Tracer("").Start(ctx, "Remote Store", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))
   291→	defer span.End()
   292→
   293→	httpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))
   294→	if err != nil {
   295→		// Errors from Client.Do are from (for example) network errors, so are
   296→		// recoverable.
   297→		return WriteResponseStats{}, RecoverableError{err, defaultBackoff}
   298→	}
   299→	defer func() {
   300→		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)
   301→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   302→	}()
   303→
   304→	// NOTE(bwplotka): Only PRW2 spec defines response HTTP headers. However, spec does not block
   305→	// PRW1 from sending them too for reliability. Support this case.
   306→	//
   307→	// TODO(bwplotka): Pass logger and emit debug on error?
   308→	// Parsing error means there were some response header values we can't parse,
   309→	// we can continue handling.
   310→	rs, _ := ParseWriteResponseStats(httpResp)
   311→
   312→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 == 2 {
   313→		return rs, nil
   314→	}
   315→
   316→	// Handling errors e.g. read potential error in the body.
   317→	// TODO(bwplotka): Pass logger and emit debug on error?
   318→	body, _ := io.ReadAll(io.LimitReader(httpResp.Body, maxErrMsgLen))
   319→	err = fmt.Errorf("server returned HTTP status %s: %s", httpResp.Status, body)
   320→
   321→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 == 5 ||
   322→		(c.retryOnRateLimit && httpResp.StatusCode == http.StatusTooManyRequests) {
   323→		return rs, RecoverableError{err, retryAfterDuration(httpResp.Header.Get("Retry-After"))}
   324→	}
   325→	return rs, err
   326→}
   327→
   328→// retryAfterDuration returns the duration for the Retry-After header. In case of any errors, it
   329→// returns the defaultBackoff as if the header was never supplied.
   330→func retryAfterDuration(t string) model.Duration {
   331→	parsedDuration, err := time.Parse(http.TimeFormat, t)
   332→	if err == nil {
   333→		s := time.Until(parsedDuration).Seconds()
   334→		return model.Duration(s) * model.Duration(time.Second)
   335→	}
   336→	// The duration can be in seconds.
   337→	d, err := strconv.Atoi(t)
   338→	if err != nil {
   339→		return defaultBackoff
   340→	}
   341→	return model.Duration(d) * model.Duration(time.Second)
   342→}
   343→
   344→// Name uniquely identifies the client.
   345→func (c *Client) Name() string {
   346→	return c.remoteName
   347→}
   348→
   349→// Endpoint is the remote read or write endpoint.
   350→func (c *Client) Endpoint() string {
   351→	return c.urlString
   352→}
   353→
   354→// Read reads from a remote endpoint. The sortSeries parameter is only respected in the case of a samples response;
   355→// chunked responses arrive already sorted by the server.
   356→func (c *Client) Read(ctx context.Context, query *prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   357→	return c.ReadMultiple(ctx, []*prompb.Query{query}, sortSeries)
   358→}
   359→
   360→// ReadMultiple reads from a remote endpoint using multiple queries in a single request.
   361→// The sortSeries parameter is only respected in the case of a samples response;
   362→// chunked responses arrive already sorted by the server.
   363→// Returns a single SeriesSet with interleaved series from all queries.
   364→func (c *Client) ReadMultiple(ctx context.Context, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   365→	c.readQueries.Inc()
   366→	defer c.readQueries.Dec()
   367→
   368→	req := &prompb.ReadRequest{
   369→		Queries:               queries,
   370→		AcceptedResponseTypes: c.acceptedResponseTypes,
   371→	}
   372→
   373→	httpResp, cancel, start, err := c.executeReadRequest(ctx, req)
   374→	if err != nil {
   375→		return nil, err
   376→	}
   377→
   378→	return c.handleReadResponse(httpResp, req, queries, sortSeries, start, cancel)
   379→}
   380→
   381→// executeReadRequest creates and executes an HTTP request for reading data.
   382→func (c *Client) executeReadRequest(ctx context.Context, req *prompb.ReadRequest) (*http.Response, context.CancelFunc, time.Time, error) {
   383→	data, err := proto.Marshal(req)
   384→	if err != nil {
   385→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("unable to marshal read request: %w", err)
   386→	}
   387→
   388→	compressed := snappy.Encode(nil, data)
   389→	httpReq, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, c.urlString, bytes.NewReader(compressed))
   390→	if err != nil {
   391→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("unable to create request: %w", err)
   392→	}
   393→	httpReq.Header.Add("Content-Encoding", "snappy")
   394→	httpReq.Header.Add("Accept-Encoding", "snappy")
   395→	httpReq.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-protobuf")
   396→	httpReq.Header.Set("User-Agent", UserAgent)
   397→	httpReq.Header.Set("X-Prometheus-Remote-Read-Version", "0.1.0")
   398→
   399→	errTimeout := fmt.Errorf("%w: request timed out after %s", context.DeadlineExceeded, c.timeout)
   400→	ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeoutCause(ctx, c.timeout, errTimeout)
   401→
   402→	ctx, span := otel.Tracer("").Start(ctx, "Remote Read", trace.WithSpanKind(trace.SpanKindClient))
   403→	defer span.End()
   404→
   405→	start := time.Now()
   406→	httpResp, err := c.Client.Do(httpReq.WithContext(ctx))
   407→	if err != nil {
   408→		cancel()
   409→		return nil, nil, time.Time{}, fmt.Errorf("error sending request: %w", err)
   410→	}
   411→
   412→	return httpResp, cancel, start, nil
   413→}
   414→
   415→// handleReadResponse processes the HTTP response and returns a SeriesSet.
   416→func (c *Client) handleReadResponse(httpResp *http.Response, req *prompb.ReadRequest, queries []*prompb.Query, sortSeries bool, start time.Time, cancel context.CancelFunc) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   417→	if httpResp.StatusCode/100 != 2 {
   418→		// Make an attempt at getting an error message.
   419→		body, _ := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   420→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   421→
   422→		cancel()
   423→		errStr := strings.Trim(string(body), "\n")
   424→		err := errors.New(errStr)
   425→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("remote server %s returned http status %s: %w", c.urlString, httpResp.Status, err)
   426→	}
   427→
   428→	contentType := httpResp.Header.Get("Content-Type")
   429→
   430→	switch {
   431→	case strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "application/x-protobuf"):
   432→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("sampled").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   433→		c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("sampled", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()
   434→		ss, err := c.handleSampledResponse(req, httpResp, sortSeries)
   435→		cancel()
   436→		return ss, err
   437→	case strings.HasPrefix(contentType, "application/x-streamed-protobuf; proto=prometheus.ChunkedReadResponse"):
   438→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("chunked").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   439→
   440→		s := NewChunkedReader(httpResp.Body, c.chunkedReadLimit, nil)
   441→		return c.handleChunkedResponseImpl(s, httpResp, queries, func(err error) {
   442→			code := strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)
   443→			if !errors.Is(err, io.EOF) {
   444→				code = "aborted_stream"
   445→			}
   446→			c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("chunked", code).Inc()
   447→			cancel()
   448→		}), nil
   449→	default:
   450→		c.readQueriesDuration.WithLabelValues("unsupported").Observe(time.Since(start).Seconds())
   451→		c.readQueriesTotal.WithLabelValues("unsupported", strconv.Itoa(httpResp.StatusCode)).Inc()
   452→		cancel()
   453→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unsupported content type: %s", contentType)
   454→	}
   455→}
   456→
   457→func (*Client) handleSampledResponse(req *prompb.ReadRequest, httpResp *http.Response, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   458→	compressed, err := io.ReadAll(httpResp.Body)
   459→	if err != nil {
   460→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response. HTTP status code: %s: %w", httpResp.Status, err)
   461→	}
   462→	defer func() {
   463→		_, _ = io.Copy(io.Discard, httpResp.Body)
   464→		_ = httpResp.Body.Close()
   465→	}()
   466→
   467→	uncompressed, err := snappy.Decode(nil, compressed)
   468→	if err != nil {
   469→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading response: %w", err)
   470→	}
   471→
   472→	var resp prompb.ReadResponse
   473→	err = proto.Unmarshal(uncompressed, &resp)
   474→	if err != nil {
   475→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("unable to unmarshal response body: %w", err)
   476→	}
   477→
   478→	if len(resp.Results) != len(req.Queries) {
   479→		return nil, fmt.Errorf("responses: want %d, got %d", len(req.Queries), len(resp.Results))
   480→	}
   481→
   482→	return combineQueryResults(resp.Results, sortSeries)
   483→}
   484→
   485→// combineQueryResults combines multiple query results into a single SeriesSet,
   486→// handling both sorted and unsorted cases appropriately.
   487→func combineQueryResults(results []*prompb.QueryResult, sortSeries bool) (storage.SeriesSet, error) {
   488→	if len(results) == 0 {
   489→		return &concreteSeriesSet{series: nil, cur: 0}, nil
   490→	}
   491→
   492→	if len(results) == 1 {
   493→		return FromQueryResult(sortSeries, results[0]), nil
   494→	}
   495→
   496→	// Multiple queries case - combine all results
   497→	if sortSeries {
   498→		// When sorting is requested, use MergeSeriesSet which can efficiently merge sorted inputs
   499→		var allSeriesSets []storage.SeriesSet
   500→		for _, result := range results {
   501→			seriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)
   502→			if err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {
   503→				return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading series from query result: %w", err)
   504→			}
   505→			allSeriesSets = append(allSeriesSets, seriesSet)
   506→		}
   507→		return storage.NewMergeSeriesSet(allSeriesSets, 0, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge), nil
   508→	}
   509→
   510→	// When sorting is not requested, just concatenate all series without using MergeSeriesSet
   511→	// since MergeSeriesSet requires sorted inputs
   512→	var allSeries []storage.Series
   513→	for _, result := range results {
   514→		seriesSet := FromQueryResult(sortSeries, result)
   515→		for seriesSet.Next() {
   516→			allSeries = append(allSeries, seriesSet.At())
   517→		}
   518→		if err := seriesSet.Err(); err != nil {
   519→			return nil, fmt.Errorf("error reading series from query result: %w", err)
   520→		}
   521→	}
   522→
   523→	return &concreteSeriesSet{series: allSeries, cur: 0}, nil
   524→}
   525→
   526→// handleChunkedResponseImpl handles chunked responses for both single and multiple queries.
   527→func (*Client) handleChunkedResponseImpl(s *ChunkedReader, httpResp *http.Response, queries []*prompb.Query, onClose func(error)) storage.SeriesSet {
   528→	// For multiple queries in chunked response, we'll still use the existing infrastructure
   529→	// but we need to provide the timestamp range that covers all queries
   530→	var minStartTs, maxEndTs int64 = math.MaxInt64, math.MinInt64
   531→
   532→	for _, query := range queries {
   533→		minStartTs = min(minStartTs, query.StartTimestampMs)
   534→		maxEndTs = max(maxEndTs, query.EndTimestampMs)
   535→	}
   536→
   537→	return NewChunkedSeriesSet(s, httpResp.Body, minStartTs, maxEndTs, onClose)
   538→}
   539→

1→// Copyright The Prometheus Authors
     2→// Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     3→// you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     4→// You may obtain a copy of the License at
     5→//
     6→// http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     7→//
     8→// Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
     9→// distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    10→// WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    11→// See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    12→// limitations under the License.
    13→
    14→package remote
    15→
    16→import (
    17→	"context"
    18→	"crypto/md5"
    19→	"encoding/hex"
    20→	"fmt"
    21→	"log/slog"
    22→	"sync"
    23→	"time"
    24→
    25→	"github.com/prometheus/client_golang/prometheus"
    26→	"github.com/prometheus/common/model"
    27→	"github.com/prometheus/common/promslog"
    28→	"go.yaml.in/yaml/v2"
    29→
    30→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/config"
    31→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/model/labels"
    32→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/scrape"
    33→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage"
    34→	"github.com/prometheus/prometheus/util/logging"
    35→)
    36→
    37→// String constants for instrumentation.
    38→const (
    39→	namespace  = "prometheus"
    40→	subsystem  = "remote_storage"
    41→	remoteName = "remote_name"
    42→	endpoint   = "url"
    43→)
    44→
    45→type ReadyScrapeManager interface {
    46→	Get() (*scrape.Manager, error)
    47→}
    48→
    49→// startTimeCallback is a callback func that return the oldest timestamp stored in a storage.
    50→type startTimeCallback func() (int64, error)
    51→
    52→// Storage represents all the remote read and write endpoints.  It implements
    53→// storage.Storage.
    54→type Storage struct {
    55→	deduper *logging.Deduper
    56→	logger  *slog.Logger
    57→	mtx     sync.Mutex
    58→
    59→	rws *WriteStorage
    60→
    61→	// For reads.
    62→	queryables             []storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable
    63→	localStartTimeCallback startTimeCallback
    64→}
    65→
    66→var _ storage.Storage = &Storage{}
    67→
    68→// NewStorage returns a remote.Storage.
    69→func NewStorage(l *slog.Logger, reg prometheus.Registerer, stCallback startTimeCallback, walDir string, flushDeadline time.Duration, sm ReadyScrapeManager, enableTypeAndUnitLabels bool) *Storage {
    70→	if l == nil {
    71→		l = promslog.NewNopLogger()
    72→	}
    73→	deduper := logging.Dedupe(l, 1*time.Minute)
    74→	logger := slog.New(deduper)
    75→
    76→	s := &Storage{
    77→		logger:                 logger,
    78→		deduper:                deduper,
    79→		localStartTimeCallback: stCallback,
    80→	}
    81→	s.rws = NewWriteStorage(s.logger, reg, walDir, flushDeadline, sm, enableTypeAndUnitLabels)
    82→	return s
    83→}
    84→
    85→func (s *Storage) Notify() {
    86→	s.rws.Notify()
    87→}
    88→
    89→// ApplyConfig updates the state as the new config requires.
    90→func (s *Storage) ApplyConfig(conf *config.Config) error {
    91→	s.mtx.Lock()
    92→	defer s.mtx.Unlock()
    93→
    94→	if err := s.rws.ApplyConfig(conf); err != nil {
    95→		return err
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	// Update read clients
    99→	readHashes := make(map[string]struct{})
   100→	queryables := make([]storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable, 0, len(conf.RemoteReadConfigs))
   101→	for _, rrConf := range conf.RemoteReadConfigs {
   102→		hash, err := toHash(rrConf)
   103→		if err != nil {
   104→			return err
   105→		}
   106→
   107→		// Don't allow duplicate remote read configs.
   108→		if _, ok := readHashes[hash]; ok {
   109→			return fmt.Errorf("duplicate remote read configs are not allowed, found duplicate for URL: %s", rrConf.URL)
   110→		}
   111→		readHashes[hash] = struct{}{}
   112→
   113→		// Set the queue name to the config hash if the user has not set
   114→		// a name in their remote write config so we can still differentiate
   115→		// between queues that have the same remote write endpoint.
   116→		name := hash[:6]
   117→		if rrConf.Name != "" {
   118→			name = rrConf.Name
   119→		}
   120→
   121→		c, err := NewReadClient(name, &ClientConfig{
   122→			URL:              rrConf.URL,
   123→			Timeout:          rrConf.RemoteTimeout,
   124→			ChunkedReadLimit: rrConf.ChunkedReadLimit,
   125→			HTTPClientConfig: rrConf.HTTPClientConfig,
   126→			Headers:          rrConf.Headers,
   127→		})
   128→		if err != nil {
   129→			return err
   130→		}
   131→
   132→		externalLabels := conf.GlobalConfig.ExternalLabels
   133→		if !rrConf.FilterExternalLabels {
   134→			externalLabels = labels.EmptyLabels()
   135→		}
   136→		queryables = append(queryables, NewSampleAndChunkQueryableClient(
   137→			c,
   138→			externalLabels,
   139→			labelsToEqualityMatchers(rrConf.RequiredMatchers),
   140→			rrConf.ReadRecent,
   141→			s.localStartTimeCallback,
   142→		))
   143→	}
   144→	s.queryables = queryables
   145→
   146→	return nil
   147→}
   148→
   149→// StartTime implements the Storage interface.
   150→func (*Storage) StartTime() (int64, error) {
   151→	return int64(model.Latest), nil
   152→}
   153→
   154→// Querier returns a storage.MergeQuerier combining the remote client queriers
   155→// of each configured remote read endpoint.
   156→// Returned querier will never return error as all queryables are assumed best effort.
   157→// Additionally all returned queriers ensure that its Select's SeriesSets have ready data after first `Next` invoke.
   158→// This is because Prometheus (fanout and secondary queries) can't handle the stream failing half way through by design.
   159→func (s *Storage) Querier(mint, maxt int64) (storage.Querier, error) {
   160→	s.mtx.Lock()
   161→	queryables := s.queryables
   162→	s.mtx.Unlock()
   163→
   164→	queriers := make([]storage.Querier, 0, len(queryables))
   165→	for _, queryable := range queryables {
   166→		q, err := queryable.Querier(mint, maxt)
   167→		if err != nil {
   168→			return nil, err
   169→		}
   170→		queriers = append(queriers, q)
   171→	}
   172→	return storage.NewMergeQuerier(nil, queriers, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge), nil
   173→}
   174→
   175→// ChunkQuerier returns a storage.MergeQuerier combining the remote client queriers
   176→// of each configured remote read endpoint.
   177→func (s *Storage) ChunkQuerier(mint, maxt int64) (storage.ChunkQuerier, error) {
   178→	s.mtx.Lock()
   179→	queryables := s.queryables
   180→	s.mtx.Unlock()
   181→
   182→	queriers := make([]storage.ChunkQuerier, 0, len(queryables))
   183→	for _, queryable := range queryables {
   184→		q, err := queryable.ChunkQuerier(mint, maxt)
   185→		if err != nil {
   186→			return nil, err
   187→		}
   188→		queriers = append(queriers, q)
   189→	}
   190→	return storage.NewMergeChunkQuerier(nil, queriers, storage.NewCompactingChunkSeriesMerger(storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)), nil
   191→}
   192→
   193→// Appender implements storage.Storage.
   194→func (s *Storage) Appender(ctx context.Context) storage.Appender {
   195→	return s.rws.Appender(ctx)
   196→}
   197→
   198→// AppenderV2 implements storage.Storage.
   199→func (s *Storage) AppenderV2(ctx context.Context) storage.AppenderV2 {
   200→	return s.rws.AppenderV2(ctx)
   201→}
   202→
   203→// LowestSentTimestamp returns the lowest sent timestamp across all queues.
   204→func (s *Storage) LowestSentTimestamp() int64 {
   205→	return s.rws.LowestSentTimestamp()
   206→}
   207→
   208→// Close the background processing of the storage queues.
   209→func (s *Storage) Close() error {
   210→	s.deduper.Stop()
   211→	s.mtx.Lock()
   212→	defer s.mtx.Unlock()
   213→	return s.rws.Close()
   214→}
   215→
   216→func labelsToEqualityMatchers(ls model.LabelSet) []*labels.Matcher {
   217→	ms := make([]*labels.Matcher, 0, len(ls))
   218→	for k, v := range ls {
   219→		ms = append(ms, &labels.Matcher{
   220→			Type:  labels.MatchEqual,
   221→			Name:  string(k),
   222→			Value: string(v),
   223→		})
   224→	}
   225→	return ms
   226→}
   227→
   228→// Used for hashing configs and diff'ing hashes in ApplyConfig.
   229→func toHash(data any) (string, error) {
   230→	bytes, err := yaml.Marshal(data)
   231→	if err != nil {
   232→		return "", err
   233→	}
   234→	hash := md5.Sum(bytes)
   235→	return hex.EncodeToString(hash[:]), nil
   236→}
   237→

    50→type startTimeCallback func() (int64, error)
    51→
    52→// Storage represents all the remote read and write endpoints.  It implements
    53→// storage.Storage.
    54→type Storage struct {
    55→	deduper *logging.Deduper
    56→	logger  *slog.Logger
    57→	mtx     sync.Mutex
    58→
    59→	rws *WriteStorage
    60→
    61→	// For reads.
    62→	queryables             []storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable
    63→	localStartTimeCallback startTimeCallback
    64→}
    65→
    66→var _ storage.Storage = &Storage{}
    67→
    68→// NewStorage returns a remote.Storage.
    69→func NewStorage(l *slog.Logger, reg prometheus.Registerer, stCallback startTimeCallback, walDir string, flushDeadline time.Duration, sm ReadyScrapeManager, enableTypeAndUnitLabels bool) *Storage {
    70→	if l == nil {
    71→		l = promslog.NewNopLogger()
    72→	}
    73→	deduper := logging.Dedupe(l, 1*time.Minute)
    74→	logger := slog.New(deduper)
    75→
    76→	s := &Storage{
    77→		logger:                 logger,
    78→		deduper:                deduper,
    79→		localStartTimeCallback: stCallback,
    80→	}
    81→	s.rws = NewWriteStorage(s.logger, reg, walDir, flushDeadline, sm, enableTypeAndUnitLabels)
    82→	return s
    83→}

    87→}

    89→// ApplyConfig updates the state as the new config requires.
    90→func (s *Storage) ApplyConfig(conf *config.Config) error {
    91→	s.mtx.Lock()
    92→	defer s.mtx.Unlock()
    93→
    94→	if err := s.rws.ApplyConfig(conf); err != nil {
    95→		return err
    96→	}
    97→
    98→	// Update read clients
    99→	readHashes := make(map[string]struct{})
   100→	queryables := make([]storage.SampleAndChunkQueryable, 0, len(conf.RemoteReadConfigs))
   101→	for _, rrConf := range conf.RemoteReadConfigs {
   102→		hash, err := toHash(rrConf)
   103→		if err != nil {
   104→			return err
   105→		}
   106→
   107→		// Don't allow duplicate remote read configs.
   108→		if _, ok := readHashes[hash]; ok {
   109→			return fmt.Errorf("duplicate remote read configs are not allowed, found duplicate for URL: %s", rrConf.URL)
   110→		}
   111→		readHashes[hash] = struct{}{}

   114→		// a name in their remote write config so we can still differentiate
   115→		// between queues that have the same remote write endpoint.
   116→		name := hash[:6]
   117→		if rrConf.Name != "" {
   118→			name = rrConf.Name
   119→		}
   120→
   121→		c, err := NewReadClient(name, &ClientConfig{
   122→			URL:              rrConf.URL,
   123→			Timeout:          rrConf.RemoteTimeout,
   124→			ChunkedReadLimit: rrConf.ChunkedReadLimit,
   125→			HTTPClientConfig: rrConf.HTTPClientConfig,
   126→			Headers:          rrConf.Headers,
   127→		})
   128→		if err != nil {
   129→			return err
   130→		}
   131→
   132→		externalLabels := conf.GlobalConfig.ExternalLabels
   133→		if !rrConf.FilterExternalLabels {
   134→			externalLabels = labels.EmptyLabels()
   135→		}
   136→		queryables = append(queryables, NewSampleAndChunkQueryableClient(
   137→			c,
   138→			externalLabels,
   139→			labelsToEqualityMatchers(rrConf.RequiredMatchers),
   140→			rrConf.ReadRecent,
   141→			s.localStartTimeCallback,
   142→		))
   143→	}
   144→	s.queryables = queryables
   145→
   146→	return nil
   147→}

   150→func (*Storage) StartTime() (int64, error) {
   151→	return int64(model.Latest), nil
   152→}
   153→
   154→// Querier returns a storage.MergeQuerier combining the remote client queriers
   155→// of each configured remote read endpoint.
   156→// Returned querier will never return error as all queryables are assumed best effort.
   157→// Additionally all returned queriers ensure that its Select's SeriesSets have ready data after first `Next` invoke.
   158→// This is because Prometheus (fanout and secondary queries) can't handle the stream failing half way through by design.
   159→func (s *Storage) Querier(mint, maxt int64) (storage.Querier, error) {
   160→	s.mtx.Lock()
   161→	queryables := s.queryables
   162→	s.mtx.Unlock()
   163→
   164→	queriers := make([]storage.Querier, 0, len(queryables))
   165→	for _, queryable := range queryables {
   166→		q, err := queryable.Querier(mint, maxt)
   167→		if err != nil {
   168→			return nil, err
   169→		}
   170→		queriers = append(queriers, q)
   171→	}
   172→	return storage.NewMergeQuerier(nil, queriers, storage.ChainedSeriesMerge), nil
   173→}
   174→

   176→// of each configured remote read endpoint.
   177→func (s *Storage) ChunkQuerier(mint, maxt int64) (storage.ChunkQuerier, error) {
   178→	s.mtx.Lock()
   179→	queryables := s.queryables
   180→	s.mtx.Unlock()
   181→
   182→	queriers := make([]storage.ChunkQuerier, 0, len(queryables))
   183→	for _, queryable := range queryables {

   185→		if err != nil {
   186→			return nil, err
   187→		}
   188→		queriers = append(queriers, q)
   189→	}
   190→	return storage.NewMergeChunkQuerier(nil, queriers, storage.NewCompactingChunkSeriesMerger(storage.ChainedSeriesMerge)), nil
   191→}

   194→func (s *Storage) Appender(ctx context.Context) storage.Appender {
   195→	return s.rws.Appender(ctx)
   196→}
   197→
   198→// AppenderV2 implements storage.Storage.
   199→func (s *Storage) AppenderV2(ctx context.Context) storage.AppenderV2 {
   200→	return s.rws.AppenderV2(ctx)
   201→}

   204→func (s *Storage) LowestSentTimestamp() int64 {
   205→	return s.rws.LowestSentTimestamp()
   206→}

   209→func (s *Storage) Close() error {
   210→	s.deduper.Stop()
   211→	s.mtx.Lock()
   212→	defer s.mtx.Unlock()
   213→	return s.rws.Close()
   214→}
